b' What I\xe2\x80\x99m Hearing: The Rock\xe2\x80\x99s Floponomics, Apple\xe2\x80\x99s Movie Search & Clint\xe2\x80\x99s Riseborough Campaign
Welcome back to What I\xe2\x80\x99m Hearing, and thanks to everyone who came to our big Stories of the Season event on Friday night. A packed house, and I met tons of nice readers and awards voters at the reception, though if I\xe2\x80\x99m honest, the special \xe2\x80\x9cWhat I\xe2\x80\x99m Drinking\xe2\x80\x9d cocktail was gross (I hate Campari). We\xe2\x80\x99ll post videos of the panels throughout the season, and our keynotes Denis Villeneuve (Dune: Part Two) and Mikey Madison (Anora) will be on The Town this week. Thanks to the fantastic Puck events team and our sponsors Polestar, Wondery, HBO and Max, and Mayer Brown. \xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c

Welcome back to What I\xe2\x80\x99m Hearing, and thanks to everyone who came to our big Stories of the Season event on Friday night. A packed house, and I met tons of nice readers and awards voters at the reception, though if I\xe2\x80\x99m honest, the special \xe2\x80\x9cWhat I\xe2\x80\x99m Drinking\xe2\x80\x9d cocktail was gross (I hate Campari). We\xe2\x80\x99ll post videos of the panels throughout the season, and our keynotes Denis Villeneuve ( Dune: Part Two ) and Mikey Madison ( Anora ) will be on The Town this week. Thanks to the fantastic Puck events team and our sponsors Polestar, Wondery, HBO and Max, and Mayer Brown.

Programming note : I discussed the Prince documentary fiasco on Today, Explained . LinkedIn News interviewed me , Lucas Shaw , and producer Craig Horlbeck about the success of The Town . This week on the show, Lucas and I debated the Disney streaming guidance; director R.J. Cutler responded to Martha Stewart \xe2\x80\x99s critique of Martha ; and Roku\xe2\x80\x99s Charlie Collier explained how he competes with Amazon, Apple, and Google. Subscribe here and here .

Also, Puck\xe2\x80\x99s annual Guide to Mirth & Merriment launched today. Click for our staff\xe2\x80\x99s (and a few V.I.P. readers\xe2\x80\x99) holiday gift ideas, including my stylish new low-speed vehicle that is definitely not a golf cart.

Not a Puck member yet? Click here . Got a news tip or an idea for me? Just reply to this email or message me anonymously on Signal at 310-804-3198.

Discussed in this issue : David Zaslav , Conan O\xe2\x80\x99Brien , Dwayne Johnson , Eddy Cue , Aaron Taylor-Johnson , Bob Iger , Debra OConnell , Elon Musk , Elizabeth Stone , Julia Ormond , Daniel Ek , Kathy Kennedy , Clint Eastwood , Matt Dentler , Jason Weinberg , Bill Kramer , Beyonc\xc3\xa9 , Roger Goodell , Ben Affleck , and\xe2\x80\xa6 the new A.I. script tracker.

But first\xe2\x80\xa6
Who Won the Week: Brendan Carr
Nexstar C.E.O. Perry Sook and the other station owners got their wish in Trump\xe2\x80\x99s pick for F.C.C. commissioner\xe2\x80\x94a \xe2\x80\x9cfree speech\xe2\x80\x9d conservative who has argued for fewer barriers to consolidation and \xe2\x80\x9crelying on competition and market forces to produce optimal outcomes.\xe2\x80\x9d He\xe2\x80\x99s also apparently anti-TikTok and wants to target Section 230, which isn\xe2\x80\x99t typically an F.C.C. issue, so\xe2\x80\xa6 maybe not so bad for Hollywood?

Runner-up : Martin Lorentzon , the Spotify co-founder, who has quietly cashed in more than $450 million worth of the company\xe2\x80\x99s high-flying stock in the past five months, per Music Business Worldwide (about $75 million more than C.E.O. Daniel Ek has minted since last summer).

Second runner-up : Julia Ormond , the actress suing CAA for knowingly sending her into abusive meetings with Harvey Weinstein . She won a ruling today allowing discovery in the case to proceed despite a separate CAA appeal. After a year of delays, it\xe2\x80\x99s finally time for Bryan Lourd , Richard Lovett , and possibly Harvey himself to sit for uncomfortable depositions. (CAA declined to comment on the ruling.)
A MESSAGE FROM OUR SPONSOR

IN SELECT THEATERS NOVEMBER 27

Starring Academy Award \xc2\xae Winner Angelina Jolie.
From Director Pablo Larra\xc3\xadn comes the re-imagining of legendary artist Maria Callas in her final days, as the diva reckons with her identity and life.

"Absolute Perfection"
-Variety

WATCH THE TRAILER

Quote of the Week
\xe2\x80\x9cSo how was everyone\xe2\x80\x99s night? :)\xe2\x80\x9d
\xe2\x80\x94 the @Peacock Twitter/X feed, trolling Netflix on Saturday morning for Friday\xe2\x80\x99s glitch-plagued broadcast of the Jake Paul - Mike Tyson fight.

A little more on this\xe2\x80\xa6

Netflix C.T.O. Elizabeth Stone attempted some damage control this weekend, emailing employees that the widespread tech issues were \xe2\x80\x9ctackled brilliantly by prioritizing stability of the stream for the majority of viewers.\xe2\x80\x9d Okay\xe2\x80\xa6 a \xe2\x80\x9cmajority of viewers\xe2\x80\x9d is fine for what was essentially a silly stunt featuring a 58-year-old boxer. But if Netflix glitches that badly during a Christmas Day NFL game with playoff implications (not to mention a Beyonc\xc3\xa9 halftime performance), Roger Goodell will personally make Stone disappear faster than she can say \xe2\x80\x9c Colin Kaepernick .\xe2\x80\x9d

Still, if there\xe2\x80\x99s a larger takeaway from Paul v. Tyson , it\xe2\x80\x99s that Netflix is now among the most powerful platforms in sports. The supposed 60 million global household audience number hasn\xe2\x80\x99t been verified by Nielsen or another third-party service, but if the actual viewership is even close to that, there\xe2\x80\x99s little reason for the major leagues to not be in business with Netflix, to the extent Netflix is willing to pay the big rights fees. I suspect that will be the next frontier in deal talks: Netflix wanting a discount for the worldwide audience it delivers. For some leagues, a haircut might be worth the exposure.

Now a few smaller items from last night\xe2\x80\x99s Governors Awards, always a chatty affair\xe2\x80\xa6
  • Could Clint get Riseboroughed? : I talked to two filmmakers who had seen Guillermo del Toro \xe2\x80\x99s social media comments about Clint Eastwood \xe2\x80\x99s Juror #2 , possibly the legend\xe2\x80\x99s final film. \xe2\x80\x9cWhy was this not released wide in the states?\xe2\x80\x9d del Toro posted last week, praising the well-reviewed film and criticizing Warner Bros.\xe2\x80\x99 tiny theatrical play with zero marketing ahead of a planned dump on Max in late December. G.D.T. is not only a best picture Oscar winner, he\xe2\x80\x99s also one of those Academy influencers who gets super involved in the awards season and champions his faves to other voters. Could this be the start of a movement among directors to net the 94-year-old Eastwood one last Oscar nomination?

    It\xe2\x80\x99s a long shot, especially if Clint doesn\xe2\x80\x99t engage at least a little with voters, though Warners says industry screenings for the film will be announced this week, writer Jonathan Abrams recently spoke at a WGA event, and star Nicholas Hoult was stumping among the awards hopefuls at the Governors Awards (though he\xe2\x80\x99s also got Nosferatu coming soon). But as we saw with To Leslie star Andrea Riseborough and her rogue support from actresses two seasons ago, such \xe2\x80\x9cgrassroots\xe2\x80\x9d campaigns are possible in the social media age. Maybe Clint should hire Riseborough\xe2\x80\x99s manager, Jason Weinberg , as a campaign consultant.

  • Disney\xe2\x80\x99s Oscars insurance policy : Near the A table, I bumped into Academy C.E.O. Bill Kramer chatting with Debra OConnell , the Disney/ABC executive who, along with Bob Iger and Dana Walden , will decide whether to renew the broadcast deal for the Oscars. Bill and Deb didn\xe2\x80\x99t want to give me an update on those early talks, but I imagine Disney\xe2\x80\x99s recent big-ticket deal to take the Grammys from CBS for the next 10 years gives OConnell some insurance in case the Academy balks at a decrease in the current $90 million-plus annual arrangement (with givebacks when ratings don\xe2\x80\x99t deliver) and wants to test the market for the top-rated awards show.

    Incidentally, that Grammys deal has been reported as everywhere from \xe2\x80\x9cmore than $500 million\xe2\x80\x9d to as much as $600 million or even more. I\xe2\x80\x99ve heard a lot of conflicting numbers\xe2\x80\x94one source told me it\xe2\x80\x99s $85 million a year by the end of the deal, and that Netflix tapped out at $75 million\xe2\x80\x94though a Disney source counters that it\xe2\x80\x99s much lower. Plus, it\xe2\x80\x99s global, inclusive of TV and streaming, and with Grammys-branded specials tacked on, so it\xe2\x80\x99s hard to compare the price to other current deals. And if Disney ponied up for the Oscars and Grammys, its ad sales team could sell both as a package and potentially float both boats higher. Since the current Academy deal ends in 2028, a big test will come in 2027, when ABC will have the Oscars, Grammys, and the Super Bowl. A triple-boondoggle to dangle in front of advertisers.

    Of course, the Oscars ratings have been in decline, despite the small uptick last year with Barbenheimer. Kramer and the other Academy people I talked to were high on the recently announced host Conan O\xe2\x80\x99Brien , who I agree will do a great job, even if he doesn\xe2\x80\x99t move the needle ratings-wise. (To that end, the Academy is praying Wicked scores a ton of nominations.) In a sign of the times, O\xe2\x80\x99Brien\xe2\x80\x99s WME reps pitched him using data from his podcast and from this spring\xe2\x80\x99s all-timer Hot Ones appearance, plus his general popularity online while not being politically polarizing. That last part is seen as key for comic hosts now, especially when conversations with four-quadrant stars Will Ferrell , Ryan Reynolds and Hugh Jackman , and others went nowhere, even after the Academy pitched a lighter load of Oscars duties.

    More importantly, the Oscars still don\xe2\x80\x99t even stream live on Disney+ or Hulu in this country, thanks to TV affiliate deals. That\xe2\x80\x99s gotta be the first deal point for the Academy, and if I were OConnell, I\xe2\x80\x99d be willing to overpay in exchange for the Academy limiting the number of Oscars presented on the live broadcast to either 15 or 20 awards (not the current 23, soon to be 24 with the addition of casting). That\xe2\x80\x99s the only way to save the 55-member board of governors from themselves and force them to produce a better show that more people actually want to watch. A show like, say, the Grammys.

  • Apple wants help with movies : Apple\xe2\x80\x99s film chief Matt Dentler was there last night, but I didn\xe2\x80\x99t have a chance to ask him what he thought of the company actively looking for a senior executive to work alongside him. Readers know Apple services chief Eddy Cue has been reevaluating the original movies initiative after misguided bets on Matthew Vaughn ( Argylle ), Greg Berlanti ( Fly Me to the Moon ), Jon Watts ( Wolfs ), and others led to box office misfires and bad press. Apple\xe2\x80\x99s not seeking a Dentler replacement, I\xe2\x80\x99m told, just a \xe2\x80\x9cstrong number two\xe2\x80\x9d (scare quotes mine), who will likely have more big-budget producing experience than Dentler, an indie sales guy and film festival programmer before getting the big job.
My Reading List\xe2\x80\xa6
Warner Discovery did a nice job spinning its NBA litigation settlement as full of cash and content concessions (broadcast rights in, um, South America, Denmark, Finland, Norway, and Sweden?!). But the fact remains that TNT is losing pro basketball in this country next year. And its side arrangement with ESPN to carry a reduced-frequency version of Inside the NBA is a straight swap, with TNT getting a couple dozen Big 12 football and basketball games, some of which were likely destined for ESPN+ Siberia. [ SBJ ]

More : A new Goldman Sachs report estimates WBD will earn $100 million a year in incremental revenue from the deal, mostly from NBA digital rights payments, plus the value of the replacement sports in keeping carriage deals and pricing. That\xe2\x80\x99s the bet-the-company question here: Will all these garbage sports rights\xe2\x80\x94sorry, lesser sports rights\xe2\x80\x94add up to carriage fees on par with the $3 per subscriber that TNT has been getting with the NBA?

More more: WBD\xe2\x80\x99s sports guru Luis Silberwasser defended the post-NBA sports aggregation strategy to Puck\xe2\x80\x99s John Ourand . [ Puck ]

As predicted by me, Disney has returned to advertising on Twitter/X, a year after Elon Musk told Bob Iger to \xe2\x80\x9cGo fuck yourself.\xe2\x80\x9d Pillars of integrity, everyone. [ Adweek ]

Kathy Kennedy blew another Star Wars release date. Note to Iger: At some point, her continued employment reflects poorly on you , not her. [ Gizmodo ]

Bill Cohan looks at how private equity could roll up cable TV networks using the DirecTV-TPG playbook. [ Puck ]

Ben Affleck \xe2\x80\x99s either hopeful or naive comments on A.I. and Hollywood at a CNBC panel are worth watching if you\xe2\x80\x99re in the pants-wetting crowd. [ YouTube ]

Speaking of A.I., The Atlantic launched a tool to see how 139,000 movies and TV shows are being used to train models by using their subtitles. [ The Atlantic ]

Now, Scott Mendelson on the lessons of this weekend\xe2\x80\x99s theatrical bomb\xe2\x80\xa6
\xe2\x80\x98Red One\xe2\x80\x99 and the New Arithmetic of Flops
The Rock\xe2\x80\x99s kidnapped Santa caper sure looks like a $253 million Christmas catastrophe for Amazon MGM Studios. It also represents the peril of a certain kind of genre and a limit to its star\xe2\x80\x99s bankability, too.
SCOTT MENDELSON
It\xe2\x80\x99s easy to dunk on Red One , the early-Christmas-season film about a North Pole security officer ( Dwayne Johnson ) recruiting a mercenary ( Chris Evans ) to help track down a kidnapped and uncomfortably buff Santa Claus ( J.K. Simmons ). The Amazon MGM Studios action-comedy cost a reported $253 million to produce, not including P&A. Its Friday-Sunday debut netted $32 million, while the film earned just $50 million in two weeks of overseas theatrical release. By most conventional theatrical measures, this is a catastrophe.

But the true verdict on the film is slightly more nuanced. Red One actually notched a solid opening for an original theatrical release, in line with other moderate successes from the past four years like The Lost City , Free Guy , and If . It also opened in line with other Rock vehicles, such as the $36 million launches of Central Intelligence , Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle , and Rampage . However, all of the aforementioned cost closer to $100 million than $200 million to produce.

Also, with an A\xe2\x80\x93 from CinemaScore and a surprisingly robust 2.93x Friday-to-weekend multiplier, Red One might yet enjoy the longer-tail success of other Christmas-themed movies released before Thanksgiving. If Red One performs like The Santa Clause 2 ($129 million after a $29 million debut in 2006), it could end up with around $142 million in North America. Meanwhile, Red One is looking at an overseas total\xe2\x80\x94courtesy of distributor-for-hire Warner Bros.\xe2\x80\x94of around $90 million. So, if you add in the international gross, the catastrophe may be downgraded to mid-range disaster before it eventually heads over to Prime Video.
The Hybrid Model
But we can\xe2\x80\x99t give Red One too much of a pass. It\xe2\x80\x99s still a $250 million movie released in a theatrical environment where even a $100 million-budgeted original is considered a major roll of the dice. Yes, the film was greenlit and budgeted for streaming before Amazon acquired MGM, and the theatrical release was ultimately considered additive to its economics. And, yes , $125 million domestic is considered a solid run for a live-action original in the current ecosystem. At the end of the day, Red One is likely to surpass the global grosses of recent Apple TV+ originals like Killers of the Flower Moon ($158 million) and Argylle ($96 million), as well as films that were initially intended for HBO Max, like Blue Beetle ($131 million).

Ironically, a $125 million\xe2\x80\x93$150 million domestic finish might have been an unvarnished domestic win for MGM, even with soft overseas numbers, had production costs been on par with the Johnson\xe2\x80\x99s successful 2010s vehicles. During his peak butts-in-seats era, the $80 million\xe2\x80\x93$130 million-budgeted movies\xe2\x80\x94 Journey 2: The Mysterious Island , San Andreas , or Rampage\xe2\x80\x94 were massive hits, with $350 million\xe2\x80\x93$475 million global cumes. So, why did Red One cost $253 million? Well, partly because the film was budgeted for streaming, meaning A-level talent got fatter paychecks upfront in lieu of performance-based box office bonuses. While it may be unfair to punish Amazon for a streaming-intended release not living up to a theatrical revenue-based profitability model, it\xe2\x80\x99s also the pool they chose to swim in.
The Rock Hits the Ceiling
Red One is just the latest example of the industry overestimating Johnson\xe2\x80\x99s bankability. Folks will show up for him to a point, but his appeal is limited unless it\xe2\x80\x99s a Fast Saga sequel or a film becomes an unto-itself sensation like Sony\xe2\x80\x99s Jumanji sequels. And that limit, of course, should be reflected in the cost. Neither the $90 million Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle nor the $120 million Jumanji: The Next Level was so expensive to produce that their respective $962 million and $800 million worldwide cumes were required to turn a profit. And the fact that Red One was made for Amazon only made it more expensive, given Johnson\xe2\x80\x99s rumored $50 million-plus buyout.

Maybe the critical lesson going forward\xe2\x80\x94beyond the fact that Dwayne Johnson should make movies that can be theatrical successes with even $350 million in worldwide receipts\xe2\x80\x94is that streaming originals should be budgeted like theatrical originals when they debut in theaters. That often means less money for talent upfront and more money spent on the movie itself. Netflix has already hinted at this change. And it does seem distasteful for streamers that willingly overpaid for A-level talent to try and make the couch competitive with theatrical stadium seating to then return to the old rules after changing the game. But, as demonstrated by Beetlejuice Beetlejuice (which went from a $147 million streaming title to a $100 million theatrical blockbuster that grossed $451 million worldwide), sometimes old school is the better school.

Nonetheless, all the caveats, disclaimers, and asterisks aside, Red One is still a $250 million movie that typifies a pattern of trying to turn almost any marquee character or brand into a franchise-friendly action-fantasy movie. This idea makes no more commercial sense in 2024 than it did in 1985 with Santa Claus: The Movie or 2013 with Jack the Giant Slayer . It\xe2\x80\x99s one thing to offer the benefit of the doubt to the kind of non-franchise, star-driven, adult-skewing genre film that Hollywood used to make before I.P. took over. It\xe2\x80\x99s another to bend over backward when tech companies and alleged disruptors make the same mistakes as their Hollywood rivals.
The Feedback
My Thursday analysis of Disney\xe2\x80\x99s bullish guidance in streaming attracted a few contrarian takes. Some examples\xe2\x80\xa6

\xe2\x80\x9cYour newsletter seems to be calling this as the moment that Disney\xe2\x80\x99s steaming has turned the corner. Maybe. But I\xe2\x80\x99d only note that their entertainment D.T.C. profit margin is in the low single digits, and their direct-to-consumer business is still $11 billion-plus in the red, not including the billions more they might be on the hook for once the haggling over the final value of Hulu is worked out. In other words, streaming is a long way from being a consistently good business for Disney. Might be someday, but Netflix has a huge head start and Disney has been running with sandbags since the beginning. It\xe2\x80\x99s really only a good business for Netflix so far.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 A journalist

\xef\xbb\xbf\xe2\x80\x9cNot sure if it\xe2\x80\x99s fair to say the right streaming strategy is \xe2\x80\x98more modest, narrowly tailored content investment, aggressive windowing, and third-party licensing\xe2\x80\x99 when the most profitable streamer does the exact opposite of all those things.\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 An executive

\xe2\x80\x9cOne of the issues with Disney financials is that it\xe2\x80\x99s masking a shit ton of streaming costs in its TV business\xe2\x80\x94all Disney Channel/Disney Junior production and localization budgets, for example. Easy to post streaming profits in this scenario\xe2\x80\xa6\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 Another executive

\xe2\x80\x9cSo everyone but Netflix and Disney are screwed?\xe2\x80\x9d \xe2\x80\x94 Yet another executive
Finally...
Sony\xe2\x80\x99s long-delayed Kraven the Hunter , which smells bad enough to derail Aaron Taylor-Johnson \xe2\x80\x99s James Bond hopes, is actually faring better than other R-rated holiday films in The Quorum\xe2\x80\x99s early tracking chart\xe2\x80\xa6
Have a great week,
Matt

Clarification : Sony\xe2\x80\x99s Ravi Ahuja was invited to Tom Rothman \xe2\x80\x99s birthday party but did not attend said party, so my characterization of Ahuja as \xe2\x80\x9cnot on the guest list\xe2\x80\x9d could have been clearer.
